title: Major Reservoir Dams of Iran — Technical & Economic Specifications
notes: 19 dams (12 constructed, 2 under construction, 5 under study) with height, reservoir capacity, generator capacity, irrigated area, year operational, and cost by dam. No existing chart_id in CHART_REGISTRY.csv covers dam-level infrastructure at all -- WDI's hydro indicators (wdi__EG.ELC.HYRO) are % of national electricity mix, not by-dam engineering/cost specs, a fundamentally different granularity. Includes the Dez Dam and Sefid Rud/Karaj/Latian/Zayandehrud dams also profiled individually elsewhere in this batch.

title: Small-Scale Irrigation Diversion Dams of Iran — Specifications
notes: 15 low-head diversion structures (crest length, height, cultivated area, utilization date) -- distinct dam category from the major reservoir dams above (no storage/hydropower, irrigation-diversion only). New topic, no existing chart_id.

title: Reservoir Water Control & Public Water Supply Forecast, by Regional Zone (4th/5th Plan)
notes: Water controlled (Mm3) and public-sector supply split (cities/industry vs agriculture) across 10 regional zones. Different granularity from WDI's national-aggregate freshwater indicators (wdi__ER.H2O.FWTL etc.) -- this is sub-national and plan-period-forecast, not a simple time series extension of those aggregates, so treated as new rather than extends.

title: Dez Multipurpose Project — Key Parameters & Cost Breakdown (World Bank Appraisal, 1960)
notes: Combines the two Dez Dam processed files (engineering/investment/schedule parameters + line-item cost breakdown by component) into one project-economics chart concept, per the task brief's explicit request to keep Dez project-economics detail as its own entry separate from the general dams comparative table above (which also includes one Dez summary row). Dez Dam = the dam listed as 'Mohammad Reza Shah-e-Pahlavi' in the comparative dams table.

title: Ghazvin (Qazvin) Area Development Project — Appraisal vs. Audited Actual Outcomes
notes: Combines the 1967 appraisal (planned cost/financing/parameters) with the 1978 completion audit (actual cost/loan disbursement/economic rate of return) into one before-and-after project-economics chart, per the task brief's explicit request. Rare instance in this project's holdings of an AUDITED actual-vs-planned outcome (rate of return collapsed from appraised 10% to ~0%) rather than just appraisal-stage projections.

title: Chromite (Chrome Ore) Production
alt_sources: bgs_wms_iran_crosscheck_1970_1974.csv (Chrome ore); usgs_comparator_argentina_turkey_metals_1968_1970.csv (Turkey has a chromite row for comparison, Argentina's is mostly NA)
notes: USGS is primary; BGS provides an independent cross-check for 1970-74 with ONE real disagreement flagged (1970: BGS 220,000t vs USGS 120,000t; 1974: BGS 140,000t vs USGS 175,000t) -- both kept as separate lines per this project's overlapping-disagreeing-sources rule, not resolved to one number.

title: Copper Production — Mine Output, Smelter Output & Refined
alt_sources: usgs_iran_commodity_narrative_highlights_1965_1980.csv (Sar Cheshmeh narrative rows provide qualitative context for the 1973-80 production jump)
notes: 3 processing stages bundled as one chart (mine/smelter/refined all present as separate 'commodity' values in the same source table) -- Iran's copper mine output jumps from ~600-5,000t/yr pre-1976 to 6,000-20,000t by 1977-78 as Sar Cheshmeh ramps up, then collapses to 3,600t by 1980 (revolution/foreign-personnel withdrawal). BGS's Copper production table was checked and has NO Iran row for 1970-74 (see mining series README).

title: Iron Ore Production (Gross Weight)
notes: 1968-1970 rows carry a preserved unit ambiguity (metric tons vs thousand metric tons, flagged not resolved -- see mining series README). BGS's Iron Ore table was checked and has NO Iran row for 1970-74.

title: Pig Iron Production
notes: Only appears in USGS from 1973 onward (Isfahan/Aryamehr steel complex startup) -- no earlier data exists in this project's holdings, a real gap not a search failure.

title: Crude Steel Production
alt_sources: imidro_capacity_growth_since_2002.csv (modern capacity bridge: 8 million t/yr capacity by 2002/03 vs this series' 1980 endpoint of 700,000t ACTUAL output)
notes: Directly connects to the Isfahan Steel Mill narrative in pahlavi_industry_series/isfahan_steel_idro_capital_goods_1972.csv (planned 750,000t stage-1 capacity by mid-1972) and usgs_iran_commodity_narrative_highlights (1975: Aryamehr complex operational at 750,000t capacity but actual 1975 crude-steel OUTPUT only 600,000t; 1980: output less than half of 1.5M capacity due to coking-coal supply problems) -- capacity vs. actual-output distinction preserved, not conflated.

title: Primary Aluminum (Ingot) Production
notes: Arak/Iralco smelter (82.5% IDRO / 12.5% Reynolds US / 5% Pakistan Govt per usgs_iran_commodity_narrative_highlights) -- output peaks 51,000t in 1975 then declines to 10,000t by 1980.

title: Lead Production — Mine Output & Smelter/Ingot Output
time_range: 1961-1980 (gaps 1966/67, 1971/72; smelter/ingot series ends 1970, no later data found)
notes: BGS and USGS closely AGREE for lead mine output (1970: both 22,940t exactly) -- a reassuring cross-validation, unlike the chromite disagreement in the same BGS crosscheck file. Smelter/ingot output has no data past 1970 in this project's holdings -- real gap.

title: Manganese Ore Production
Zinc Production (Mine Output, Metal Content)
notes: BGS's Zinc production table was checked and has NO Iran row for 1970-74 (confirmed absence, see mining series README).

title: Barite Production
Gypsum Production
notes: No earlier data in this project's holdings (1961-65 volume's Iran chapter did not report gypsum) -- real gap, not omitted.

title: Salt (Rock) Production
Sulfur Production (Refined + Elemental Byproduct)
notes: Source terminology shifts mid-series (see mining series README) -- preserved as printed per row, not force-normalized.

title: Coal Production (Non-Oil)
notes: Non-oil coal specifically -- crude/refined petroleum are covered by other datasets already in this project (deliberately excluded from this source per its own manifest).

title: Coke Production
Cement (Hydraulic) Production
notes: SPLICE: the Pahlavi-era industrial-production table's Cement row (65.0 -> 410.1 thousand metric tons, 1954/55-1958/59) and the USGS mining series' Cement row (745 -> 9,000 thousand metric tons, 1961-1980) are the SAME commodity in the SAME unit (thousand metric tons) and join into one plausible continuous growth trajectory (410 in 1958/59 -> 745 by 1961 is a reasonable 2-3 year gap-adjusted growth rate). Documented here as the join; both underlying files kept as separate raw sources, never merged/edited in data/raw/.

title: Sar Cheshmeh Copper Project — Development Narrative & Modern Production
notes: Qualitative narrative (1970 formal $330M approval -> 1975 construction 15% complete -> 1980 stalled by revolution, output redirected to domestic market) bridged to a modern quantitative production snapshot (concentrate/anode/cathode/molybdenum/gold-silver byproduct tonnage, 2021/22). No continuous annual series exists in this project's holdings for the intervening 1981-2020 period -- a real, large gap, logged not papered over. Task brief referenced a standalone 'Sar Cheshmeh narrative dataset' that does not exist as its own raw folder -- this chart entry IS that material, assembled from the two sources that actually contain it (see iran_mining_series/README.md).

title: Newly Discovered Mineral Reserves — Valuation by Commodity (IMIDRO)
notes: Valuation (quantity x assumed unit price), not a production or reserves-in-the-ground series -- distinct concept from the production charts above, flagged in README to avoid conflation. $27.8B cumulative total, dominated by copper ($16.6B) and iron ore ($5.86B).

title: Steel/Copper/Aluminum Production CAPACITY Growth Since IMIDRO's Founding (2002-2021)
notes: Installed CAPACITY (steel 8->43.5M t/yr, copper cathode 190k->450k t/yr, aluminium billet 216k->770k t/yr), not actual annual output -- cross-referenced as a bridge point in the corresponding ACTUAL-production chart entries above (crude steel, aluminum) but kept as its own chart since capacity and output are different metrics that should not be silently merged into one series.

title: Processed Tea Output (Manufacturing, not Raw Leaf)
notes: This is PROCESSED/manufactured tea output (1000 metric tons), a distinct concept from FAOSTAT's 'Tea leaves — Production' (faostat__Tea leaves__production, raw agricultural leaf) and from this project's already-existing (but not yet registry-staged) hand-curated specialty_goods_series/tea_1895_1984.csv, which covers the same processed-tea-industry topic across a wider 1895-1984 span -- likely splices with that file once BOTH are staged together in a future registry pass; noted here so that future pass doesn't create a duplicate chart_id.

title: Processed (Refined) Sugar Output
notes: Refined sugar output (1000 metric tons), distinct from FAOSTAT's raw sugar-cane/beet production charts. Same cross-reference note as the tea chart above applies to specialty_goods_series/sugar_1890_2000.csv.

title: Textile Cloth Output by Fiber Type (Cotton, Woolen, Jute, Silk)
underlying_codes: pahlavi_industry_series/industrial_production_1954_1959.csv (commodity in {Cotton cloth, Woolen cloth, Jute cloth, Silk cloth})
notes: Physical cloth output in million metres -- a different metric from iran_automotive_textile_series/textile_sector_pahlavi_to_2002.csv's spindle-count/employment/cotton-tonnage series, but the same broad topic; cross-reference both when charting the textile sector. Cotton cloth output nearly doubled 1957/58-1958/59 alone (63.0 -> 107.0 million metres).

title: Cigarette Production
alt_sources: specialty_goods_series/tobacco_monopoly_1890_1995.csv (not yet registry-staged, covers tobacco LEAF/cultivation, not manufactured cigarettes)
notes: Manufactured cigarette output (million units) under the Dokhaniyat state tobacco monopoly -- distinct product stage from the existing tobacco-leaf-cultivation figures in specialty_goods_series.

title: Miscellaneous Light Manufacturing Output (Matches, Soap, Rubber Shoes, Glass, Soft Drinks)
underlying_codes: pahlavi_industry_series/industrial_production_1954_1959.csv (commodity in {Matches, Soap, Rubber shoes, Glass, Soft drinks})
notes: 5 minor commodities bundled into one chart entry rather than 5 separate entries, given their thin 5-year single-table snapshot nature -- rubber shoes shows the most dramatic growth (0.6 -> 8.2 million pairs, +1,267%) reflecting early import-substitution success in that specific product.

title: Industrial Import Dependence — Share of Imported Inputs in Sales Value, by Product
notes: 33 products across 5 categories -- a rare pre-revolution import-substitution-progress snapshot with no equivalent anywhere else in this project's holdings.

title: Isfahan Steel Mill (Aryamehr/NISC) & IDRO Capital-Goods Plants — Construction & Financing Narrative
time_range: 1972 report describing construction staged through the late 1970s
notes: QUALITATIVE/narrative, not a numeric time series -- not directly chartable, included in the registry as documentary context for the numeric Isfahan/steel figures in iran_crude_steel_production_1973_1980 and iran_pig_iron_production_1973_1980 above. Covers USSR barter financing, 3-stage capacity plan, and IDRO's 4 capital-goods plants (Arak aluminum/Reynolds, Arak heavy engineering, Tabriz machine tools/Czechoslovakia, Tabriz agricultural equipment/Romania).

title: National Gasoline Consumption
notes: SPLICE: two files join at 1962 (first file ends 1961/62 at 645.0 million liters; second file begins 1962 at 4.22 million barrels -- UNIT CHANGES from liters to barrels between the two sources, not a data error; approximate conversion 1 barrel = 158.99 liters implies ~671 million liters for 1962, consistent with continued growth from 645.0 in 1961/62). No existing WDI chart_id covers gasoline consumption specifically (wdi__EG.USE.PCAP.KG.OE is total energy use per capita in oil-equivalent, a different and broader concept).

title: Railways, Goods Transported (Freight Traffic)
time_range: 1953/54-1960/61 (WDI Iran coverage for the same indicator only starts 1995 -- 34-year gap, no connecting data found)
notes: SAME unit as WDI's IS.RRS.GOOD.MT.K6 (million ton-km) -- genuine splice candidate, though with a large undocumented gap between this table's 1960/61 endpoint (2,145 million ton-km) and WDI's 1995 start. Also includes freight tonnage (1000 tons) which WDI does not track at all -- that column is additive beyond the pure time-extension.

title: Railways, Passengers Carried (Passenger Traffic)
time_range: 1953/54-1960/61 (WDI Iran coverage for the same indicator only starts 1995 -- 34-year gap, no connecting data found)
notes: SAME unit as WDI's IS.RRS.PASG.KM (million passenger-km) -- genuine splice candidate, same 34-year gap caveat as the freight chart above. Shows a striking pre-WDI-era jump: passenger-km more than doubled from 443M (1956/57) to 995M (1957/58) then nearly doubled again to 1,961M by 1959/60.

title: Iranian State Railways — Income & Expense Account
notes: Financial (revenue/expense/operating-balance, million rials), not physical-traffic -- a different concept from the two traffic-extends charts above and from anything WDI tracks for railways (WDI has no railway-agency financial-performance indicator). Operating margin compresses from ~35% to ~26% of revenue across the period as expenses outgrow revenue growth.

title: Road Vehicle Registration by Type (Cars, Buses, Trucks, Motorcycles)
notes: SPLICE: two-point comparison (1955/56: 64,123 total; 1960/61: 148,375 total) joins cleanly into the continuous annual series (1962: 149,900 total -- consistent with the 1960/61 endpoint). No existing WDI chart_id covers Iran vehicle registration/fleet counts at all (confirmed: wdi__IS.VEH.NVEH.P3/PCAR.P3/TOTL.P3 all have ZERO Iran observations in this project's harmonized WDI data -- genuinely new topic, not an extends case, despite the conceptual similarity to a 'vehicles per capita' indicator WDI does define elsewhere.

title: National Motor Vehicle Production (incl. Paykan/Iran Khodro Milestones)
notes: PRODUCTION (manufacturing output), distinct from the vehicle REGISTRATION/fleet-count chart above (iran_vehicle_registration_by_type_1955_1972) -- a stock-vs-flow distinction, kept as separate charts. Shows the Iran-Iraq War-era collapse (1990: 44,665 vs 1980: 161,000) and post-war recovery to 817,200 by 2005. Mostly secondary-sourced (Wikipedia) -- see automotive/textile series README caveats for the one flagged unresolved discrepancy (1979 Paykan-only estimate vs 1980 industry-wide total, not directly comparable).

title: Textile Sector Capacity, Employment & Cotton Production
notes: Spindle/loom counts, factory counts, sector employment, and raw cotton production (metric tons) -- a capacity/input-side view of the textile sector, distinct from the physical cloth-OUTPUT-in-metres chart (iran_textile_cloth_output_by_fiber_1954_1959) above; the two should be read together, not conflated. Mostly secondary-sourced (Encyclopaedia Iranica via a working mirror, canonical iranicaonline.org domain blocks direct fetch) -- see automotive/textile series README caveats.
